summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by expressing his love for the congregation and shared a word he received from God at 2 a.m.: sometimes God's word comes as a seed, and other times it comes as a sword. Today's message, 'Firm Foundation,' was the sword—a direct, convicting call to examine what we are building our lives upon. Pastor Travis taught from Matthew 7, where Jesus contrasts the wise man who builds his house on the rock with the foolish man who builds on sand. The key to a firm foundation is not just hearing God's word but doing it. He warned that many build their lives on sinking sand—relationships, success, pleasure, or the ever-changing standards of the world—which leads to a great fall. The sermon emphasized that the truth of God's Word, embodied in Jesus Christ, is the only unchanging foundation. Pastor Travis highlighted the dangers of being led by our own desires, which can entrap us and lead to sin and death, referencing James 1 and the warnings about the last days in 2 Timothy. He encouraged the church to be like Daniel, who chose to pray to God despite a death decree, trusting God rather than conforming to the world. The message concluded with a call from Romans 12:2 to not conform but be transformed, and to let our light shine before others as a testimony to Christ.
mainPoints: A firm foundation for life is built by both hearing and doing God's Word, not by the shifting standards of the world.
Our own desires can entice and trap us, leading to sin and spiritual death, making it critical to build our lives on the unchanging truth of God.
In a world that demands compliance, we must choose, like Daniel, to trust God and not conform, allowing our transformed lives to shine as a light for Christ.

versesMentioned.text: For the word of God is alive and active. Sharper than any double-edged sword, it penetrates even to dividing soul and spirit, joints and marrow; it judges the thoughts and attitudes of the heart.
All Scripture is God-breathed and is useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting and training in righteousness, so that the servant of God may be thoroughly equipped for every good work.

Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock. The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it did not fall, because it had its foundation on the rock. But everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand. The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell with a great crash.
You believe that there is one God. Good! Even the demons believe that—and shudder.

Therefore, get rid of all moral filth and the evil that is so prevalent and humbly accept the word planted in you, which can save you. Do not merely listen to the word, and so deceive yourselves. Do what it says.

But each person is tempted when they are dragged away by their own evil desire and enticed. Then, after desire has conceived, it gives birth to sin; and sin, when it is full-grown, gives birth to death.
For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

But mark this: There will be terrible times in the last days. People will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, proud, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy, without love, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, not lovers of the good, treacherous, rash, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God— having a form of godliness but denying its power. Have nothing to do with such people.

For the time will come when people will not put up with sound doctrine. Instead, to suit their own desires, they will gather around them a great number of teachers to say what their itching ears want to hear. They will turn their ears away from the truth and turn aside to myths.
For the flesh desires what is contrary to the Spirit, and the Spirit what is contrary to the flesh. They are in conflict with each other, so that you are not to do whatever you want.
The heart is deceitful above all things and beyond cure. Who can understand it?
There is a way that appears to be right, but in the end it leads to death.

Now when Daniel learned that the decree had been published, he went home to his upstairs room where the windows opened toward Jerusalem. Three times a day he got down on his knees and prayed, giving thanks to his God, just as he had done before.
Be alert and of sober mind. Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.
Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will.

You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden. Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house. In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.
for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,

Monday.scripture: Matthew 7:24 - 'Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.'
Monday.action: Identify one instruction from Scripture you know but haven't been practicing. Commit to one small, specific action to obey it today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to not just be a hearer of Your Word today, but a doer. Give me the strength to put my faith into action.

Tuesday.scripture: James 1:14-15 - 'But each person is tempted when they are dragged away by their own evil desire and enticed...'
Tuesday.action: Reflect on a recurring temptation. Ask yourself: what desire is behind it? Write it down and pray for God to align that desire with His truth.
Tuesday.prayer: God, reveal the desires of my heart. Help me to see where I am being enticed and give me the wisdom to turn toward You instead.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Timothy 4:3-4 - 'For the time will come when people will not put up with sound doctrine...'
Wednesday.action: Read a passage from the Bible (e.g., Psalm 119:89). Meditate on the fact that God's Word does not change, even when the world does.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, thank You that Your Word is eternal and true. Anchor my heart in Your unchanging truth today, and protect me from being swayed by popular opinion.

Thursday.scripture: Daniel 6:10 - '...Three times a day he got down on his knees and prayed, giving thanks to his God, just as he had done before.'
Thursday.action: Set aside three specific times today to stop and pray, even if just for a minute. Be consistent, like Daniel, regardless of circumstances.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, give me the courage and discipline of Daniel. I choose to prioritize prayer today, trusting You above any pressure or fear.

Friday.scripture: Romans 12:2 - 'Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind.'
Friday.action: In one area where you feel pressure to 'blend in' (at work, online, with friends), consciously choose a thought, word, or action that honors Christ.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, renew my mind. Transform me from the inside out so that I reflect Christ and do not simply conform to the world around me.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 5:16 - 'In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.'
Saturday.action: Intentionally perform one act of kindness or speak one word of encouragement today with the simple motive of pointing someone toward God's love.
Saturday.prayer: God, may my life be a light that points others to You. Use my simple actions today to bring glory to Your name.
